channel.title: Insights · Life Portfolio Blog
channel.description: Discover, live it out, leave a trace. Self-management essays, Only One Report how-to guides, and 21-day routine designs.

item.title: MBTI is free — why pay $8.99? A 5-point quantitative comparison · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Type label vs one-line mission, strengths and growth on one page, three first actions this week, 3-month criteria, 1-year vision — what each instrument actually answers.

item.title: Career questions in your 30s — what to do before another personality test · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Career drift in your 30s is rarely solved by another type test. The five lines that actually move the decision: mission, vision, strengths, three first actions, and a 3-week routine.

item.title: Restarting after burnout — begin from mission, not output · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: The first mistake after burnout is “work harder.” The truer first line is “who was I doing this for?” A 7-day, low-intensity recovery sequence that starts from mission.

item.title: Self-development for working adults — skills or mission first? · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Self-development fails not from laziness but from buying skills before naming the mission. A four-layer model — vision, mission, strengths, skills — and a one-line filter before your next course purchase.

item.title: Before you buy — 7 things to check (FAQ) · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Delivery time, refund policy, age 14 self-declaration, re-download, group plans, data retention, and what we mean by “accuracy.” The seven most common pre-purchase questions on one page.

item.title: Three weeks for $8.99 vs ten books — a time and cost ROI · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Ten self-help books cost ~$150 and 30–50 hours. Can $8.99 and 15 minutes stand in their seat? A four-axis comparison: cost, time, output format, and calendar shift.

item.title: Matthew 24:45 and 28:18-20 — the theological roots of Life Portfolio · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Why a self-management tool quotes Matt 24:45 and 28:18-20. The 7 leadership domains, the 5 biblical leadership archetypes, and the 4-floor vision house behind the booklet.

item.title: Monthly review + designing the next three weeks · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: A 30-minute, 4-step end-of-month review plus a single card for the next three weeks. Those 30 minutes set the weight of the next month.

item.title: Ten years of personality tests vs Life Portfolio — what remains · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: What remains in your hand after ten years on each side: stacked one-line missions, 120 review cards, decision speed, and a booklet you can lend to others.

item.title: One mission, one report — exactly what you receive after payment · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: What exactly do you receive for $8.99? An anonymized walkthrough (Founder J) of a real Life Portfolio report and the matching execution program — cover, 5-axis insights, 3-week routine, 3-month structure, and 1-year vision.

item.title: How to live your report next week — A 7-day Week 1 checklist · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: The PDF is the start, not the finish. The week most people fail is the one right after the report arrives. A 7-day Week 1 checklist — 5 to 10 minutes a day, one place where your mission meets your week.

item.title: Beyond MBTI — The Next Step After Self-Awareness · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: Personality tests describe who you are. The next step is what you'll live out — your mission, vision, strengths in one line, plus three first actions and a 3-week routine.

item.title: From Mission to Daily Action — A 21-Day Framework · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: A simple 21-day framework that turns a personal mission statement into daily action — one line, three first actions in if-then form, and a three-week routine.

item.title: Why personality tests miss the point — A self-management view · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: MBTI, Enneagram, DISC and Big Five describe who you are — but never tell you what you're meant to live out. Why mission, vision and strengths must be lined up before any 'type' becomes useful.

item.title: How to use your Only One Report for the next 21 days — A 4-step guide · Life Portfolio Insights
item.description: The Only One Report is the start, not the finish. A 4-step guide to wire mission, vision and strengths into your daily life over 21 days — print and post, three first actions on the calendar, weekly 5-minute review, and one-word refinement.
